title: DPmixGPD: Model specification and posterior computation
text: DPM bulk model can written allocation form: yi∣zi,{Θj}∼k(yi∣Θzi),zi∣𝐰∼Categorical(w1,w2,…), y_i \mid z_i,\{\Theta_j\} \;\sim\; k(y_i \mid \Theta_{z_i}), \qquad z_i \mid \boldsymbol{w} \;\sim\; \mathrm{Categorical}(w_1,w_2,\dots), Θj∣H∼iidH,H∼DP(κ,H0), \Theta_j \mid H \;\overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim}\; H, \qquad H \;\sim\; \mathrm{DP}(\kappa, H_0),  k(⋅∣Θj)k(\cdot\mid \Theta_j) chosen kernel family (Normal, Gamma, Lognormal, Amoroso, etc.), H0H_0 base measure kernel parameters, κ\kappa controls clustering (smaller κ\kappa yields fewer active components; larger κ\kappa yields ) (Ferguson 1973). induced marginal bulk density (infinite) mixture: fDPM(y)=∑j=1∞wjk(y∣Θj). f_{\mathrm{DPM}}(y) \;=\; \sum_{j=1}^{\infty} w_j \, k(y \mid \Theta_j).

title: DPmixGPD: Model specification and posterior computation
text: DPmixGPD supports two computational backends: stick-breaking (backend="sb") CRP (backend="crp"). stick-breaking, Sethuramanâ€™s constructive form (Sethuraman 1994) wj=Vj∏ℓ<j(1−Vℓ),Vj∼iidBeta(1,κ),Θj∼iidH0. w_j = V_j \prod_{\ell<j}(1-V_\ell),  \qquad V_j \overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim} \mathrm{Beta}(1,\kappa), \qquad \Theta_j \overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim} H_0. computation, DPmixGPD uses finite truncation level JJ (components) fDPM(y)≈∑j=1Jwjk(y∣Θj), f_{\mathrm{DPM}}(y) \;\approx\; \sum_{j=1}^{J} w_j \, k(y \mid \Theta_j),  final weight absorbing leftover mass. standard blocked-Gibbs approximation stick-breaking priors (Ishwaran James 2001).

title: DPmixGPD: Model specification and posterior computation
text: Define allocation counts nj=∑=1N𝕀(zi=j),mj=∑=1N𝕀(zi>j). n_j = \sum_{=1}^N \mathbb{}(z_i=j), \qquad m_j = \sum_{=1}^N \mathbb{}(z_i>j).  stick-breaking variables conjugate full conditional Vj∣𝐳,κ∼Beta(1+nj,κ+mj), V_j \mid \boldsymbol{z},\kappa \;\sim\; \mathrm{Beta}(1+n_j,\;\kappa+m_j),  weights reconstructed wj=Vj∏ℓ<j(1−Vℓ). w_j = V_j \prod_{\ell<j}(1-V_\ell). update one reason blocked Gibbs convenient: yields tractable conditional distributions weights truncation.

title: DPmixGPD: Model specification and posterior computation
text: Let threshold u(𝐱)u(\boldsymbol{x}) (constant uu). y>u(𝐱)y>u(\boldsymbol{x}), define exceedance e=y−u(𝐱)e = y-u(\boldsymbol{x}). GPD model exceedances Y∣Y>u(𝐱),𝐱∼GPD(u(𝐱),σ(𝐱),ξ), Y \mid Y>u(\boldsymbol{x}),\boldsymbol{x} \;\sim\; \mathrm{GPD}\big(u(\boldsymbol{x}),\sigma(\boldsymbol{x}),\xi\big),  scale σ(𝐱)>0\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})>0 shape ξ∈ℝ\xi\\mathbb{R}. density fGPD(y∣u(𝐱),σ(𝐱),ξ)={1σ(𝐱)(1+ξy−u(𝐱)σ(𝐱))−1/ξ−1,ξ≠0,1σ(𝐱)exp⁡(−y−u(𝐱)σ(𝐱)),ξ=0, f_{\mathrm{GPD}}(y \mid u(\boldsymbol{x}),\sigma(\boldsymbol{x}),\xi) = \begin{cases} \dfrac{1}{\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})} \left(1+\xi\dfrac{y-u(\boldsymbol{x})}{\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})}\right)^{-1/\xi-1}, & \xi\neq 0,\\[10pt] \dfrac{1}{\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})}\exp\!\left(-\dfrac{y-u(\boldsymbol{x})}{\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})}\right), & \xi = 0, \end{cases}  support constraint 1+ξ(y−u(𝐱))/σ(𝐱)>01+\xi(y-u(\boldsymbol{x}))/\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})>0. CDF FGPD(y∣u(𝐱),σ(𝐱),ξ)={1−(1+ξy−u(𝐱)σ(𝐱))−1/ξ,ξ≠0,1−exp⁡(−y−u(𝐱)σ(𝐱)),ξ=0. F_{\mathrm{GPD}}(y \mid u(\boldsymbol{x}),\sigma(\boldsymbol{x}),\xi) = \begin{cases} 1-\left(1+\xi\dfrac{y-u(\boldsymbol{x})}{\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})}\right)^{-1/\xi}, & \xi\neq 0,\\[10pt] 1-\exp\!\left(-\dfrac{y-u(\boldsymbol{x})}{\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})}\right), & \xi = 0. \end{cases} use GPD exceedances justified classical peaks--threshold limit theory (Balkema Haan 1974; Pickands 1975; Coles 2001).

title: DPmixGPD: Model specification and posterior computation
text: DPmixGPD splices bulk DPM GPD tail u(𝐱)u(\boldsymbol{x}) defining full conditional density f(y∣𝐱)={fDPM(y∣𝐱),y≤u(𝐱),(1−FDPM(u(𝐱)∣𝐱))fGPD(y∣u(𝐱),σ(𝐱),ξ),y>u(𝐱). f(y \mid \boldsymbol{x}) = \begin{cases} f_{\mathrm{DPM}}(y \mid \boldsymbol{x}), & y \le u(\boldsymbol{x}),\\[6pt] \Big(1 - F_{\mathrm{DPM}}(u(\boldsymbol{x}) \mid \boldsymbol{x})\Big)\; f_{\mathrm{GPD}}(y \mid u(\boldsymbol{x}),\sigma(\boldsymbol{x}),\xi), & y > u(\boldsymbol{x}). \end{cases}  multiplicative factor SDPM(u(𝐱)∣𝐱)=1−FDPM(u(𝐱)∣𝐱) S_{\mathrm{DPM}}(u(\boldsymbol{x}) \mid \boldsymbol{x}) = 1 - F_{\mathrm{DPM}}(u(\boldsymbol{x}) \mid \boldsymbol{x})  ensures model proper density full support tail probability mass matches bulk survival beyond threshold. Equivalently, spliced CDF F(y∣𝐱)={FDPM(y∣𝐱),y≤u(𝐱),FDPM(u(𝐱)∣𝐱)+(1−FDPM(u(𝐱)∣𝐱))FGPD(y∣u(𝐱),σ(𝐱),ξ),y>u(𝐱). F(y \mid \boldsymbol{x}) = \begin{cases} F_{\mathrm{DPM}}(y \mid \boldsymbol{x}), & y \le u(\boldsymbol{x}),\\[6pt] F_{\mathrm{DPM}}(u(\boldsymbol{x}) \mid \boldsymbol{x}) + \Big(1 - F_{\mathrm{DPM}}(u(\boldsymbol{x}) \mid \boldsymbol{x})\Big)\; F_{\mathrm{GPD}}(y \mid u(\boldsymbol{x}),\sigma(\boldsymbol{x}),\xi), & y > u(\boldsymbol{x}). \end{cases} posterior functionals DPmixGPD (predictive density/CDF, quantiles, exceedance probabilities) derived single definition. Later vignettes reference section rather restating .

title: DPmixGPD: Model specification and posterior computation
text: DPmixGPD constructs NIMBLE model runs MCMC using NIMBLEâ€™s samplers. broad terms, posterior sampler alternates updates : allocations {zi}\{z_i\}, component parameters {Θj}\{\Theta_j\} (including regression coefficients link mode), mixture weights (SB) partitions (CRP), concentration κ\kappa, (enabled) tail parameters {u(⋅),σ(⋅),ξ}\{u(\cdot),\sigma(\cdot),\xi\}.

title: DPmixGPD: Causal treatment effects (ATE/QTE) via two-arm outcome models
text: Let T∈{0,1}T\\{0,1\} denote binary treatment indicator Y(0),Y(1)Y(0), Y(1) potential outcomes. observed outcome Y=TY(1)+(1−T)Y(0). Y = T\,Y(1) + (1-T)\,Y(0).  covariate vector 𝐱\boldsymbol{x}, define conditional CDFs quantiles Fa(y∣𝐱)=Pr⁡{Y()≤y∣𝐗=𝐱},Qa(τ∣𝐱)=Fa−1(τ∣𝐱),τ∈(0,1), F_a(y\mid \boldsymbol{x}) = \Pr\{Y()\le y \mid \boldsymbol{X}=\boldsymbol{x}\}, \qquad Q_a(\tau\mid \boldsymbol{x}) = F_a^{-1}(\tau\mid \boldsymbol{x}), \quad \tau\(0,1),  conditional quantile treatment effect QTE(τ∣𝐱)=Q1(τ∣𝐱)−Q0(τ∣𝐱). \mathrm{QTE}(\tau\mid \boldsymbol{x}) = Q_1(\tau\mid \boldsymbol{x}) - Q_0(\tau\mid \boldsymbol{x}).  conditional mean effect (ATE 𝐱\boldsymbol{x}) ATE(𝐱)=𝔼{Y(1)∣𝐱}−𝔼{Y(0)∣𝐱}. \mathrm{ATE}(\boldsymbol{x}) = \mathbb{E}\{Y(1)\mid \boldsymbol{x}\} - \mathbb{E}\{Y(0)\mid \boldsymbol{x}\}. DPmixGPDâ€™s qte() ate() functions compute treated-minus-control functionals fitted causal object. supply newdata, obtain effects evaluated covariate profiles; otherwise DPmixGPD defaults training design XX present.

title: DPmixGPD: Causal treatment effects (ATE/QTE) via two-arm outcome models
text: DPmixGPD follows standard â€œselection observablesâ€ identification strategy (Rubin causal model) (Rubin 1974). identifying assumptions : Consistency / SUTVA: observed outcome equals potential outcome received treatment; interference units. Unconfoundedness: {Y(0),Y(1)}⟂T∣𝐗. \{Y(0),Y(1)\} \perp T \mid \boldsymbol{X}. Overlap (positivity): 0<e(𝐱)<1for covariate values interest, 0 < e(\boldsymbol{x}) < 1 \quad \text{covariate values interest,}  e(𝐱)=Pr⁡(T=1∣𝐗=𝐱)e(\boldsymbol{x})=\Pr(T=1\mid \boldsymbol{X}=\boldsymbol{x}) propensity score. assumptions, Fa(y∣𝐱)=Pr⁡(Y≤y∣T=,𝐗=𝐱), F_a(y\mid \boldsymbol{x}) = \Pr(Y\le y \mid T=,\boldsymbol{X}=\boldsymbol{x}),  causal effects can estimated modeling observed conditional outcome distribution within treatment arm.

what: Why include a propensity score module?
title: DPmixGPD: Causal treatment effects (ATE/QTE) via two-arm outcome models
text: Rosenbaum Rubin showed propensity score balancing score: adjusting e(𝐱)e(\boldsymbol{x}) sufficient remove bias due observed covariates (Rosenbaum Rubin 1983). DPmixGPD offers lightweight PS model can pass PS-derived covariate outcome arms additional predictor. optional: set PS = FALSE skip propensity score estimation use XX outcome models; set PS = "logit" / "probit" / "naive" estimate PS include outcome arms. Important details: ps_scale = "prob" uses posterior summary ê(𝐱)\hat e(\boldsymbol{x}) directly. ps_scale = "logit" uses logit(ê(𝐱))\mathrm{logit}(\hat e(\boldsymbol{x})) (often numerically friendlier covariate). ps_summary = "mean" "median" chooses posterior e(𝐱)e(\boldsymbol{x}) summarized passed outcome arms. ps_clamp clamps ê(𝐱)\hat e(\boldsymbol{x}) (ε,1−ε)(\varepsilon, 1-\varepsilon) avoid infinities logit transform.

title: Conditional DP Mixture Models
text: Let yi∈ℝy_i \\mathbb{R} outcome 𝐱∈ℝp\boldsymbol{x}_i \\mathbb{R}^p covariate vector. truncated stick-breaking (SB) Dirichlet process mixture, DPmixGPD uses yi∣zi=j,𝐱∼k(yi∣𝛉j(𝐱)),zi∼Categorical(w1,…,wJ),   y_i \mid z_i=j,\boldsymbol{x}_i \sim k\!\left(y_i \mid \boldsymbol{\theta}_j(\boldsymbol{x}_i)\right), \qquad   z_i \sim \mathrm{Categorical}(w_1,\dots,w_J),  stick-breaking weights vj∼Beta(1,α),w1=v1,wj=vj∏ℓ<j(1−vℓ)(j≥2),α∼(prior).   v_j \sim \mathrm{Beta}(1,\alpha),\quad   w_1=v_1,\quad   w_j=v_j\prod_{\ell<j}(1-v_\ell)\ (j\ge 2),\quad   \alpha \sim \text{(prior)}.  DPM idea classic DP mixtures, applied conditional distributions; see, e.g., (escobar1995?; muller1996?). key difference unconditional vignette 𝛉j(𝐱)\boldsymbol{\theta}_j(\boldsymbol{x}) may depend 𝐱\boldsymbol{x}. DPmixGPD implements covariate dependence per-parameter modes: mode = "dist": parameter random (drawn prior) depend XX. mode = "fixed": parameter fixed constant value. mode = "link": parameter depends XX GLM-style link, component-specific regression coefficients. set separately bulk parameter (kernel parameter), selected tail parameters GPD = TRUE.

title: Conditional DP Mixture Models
text: bulk parameter ϑ\vartheta assigned mode = "link", DPmixGPD uses linear predictor link function: ηϑ,ij=𝐱⊤𝛃ϑ,j,ϑij=gϑ(ηϑ,ij).   \eta_{\vartheta,ij} = \boldsymbol{x}_i^\top \boldsymbol{\beta}_{\vartheta,j},\qquad   \vartheta_{ij} = g_\vartheta(\eta_{\vartheta,ij}).  𝛃ϑ,j\boldsymbol{\beta}_{\vartheta,j} component-specific (indexed mixture component jj), different mixture components can represent different conditional regimes. Supported link functions include: "identity": ϑ=η\vartheta = \eta "exp": ϑ=exp⁡(η)\vartheta = \exp(\eta) (useful positive parameters) "softplus": ϑ=log⁡(1+exp⁡(η))\vartheta = \log(1+\exp(\eta)) (also enforces positivity) "power": ϑ=ηκ\vartheta = \eta^{\kappa} (requires link_power = kappa; use care η\eta can negative) best default strictly-positive parameters typically "exp" "softplus".

title: Conditional DP Mixture Models
text: GPD = TRUE, DPmixGPD uses GPD tail threshold (see Model fundamentals vignette splice GPD density). conditional variants, following tail parameters can depend XX: gpd$threshold: fixed, dist, link gpd$tail_scale: fixed, dist, link gpd$tail_shape: fixed dist (link mode) Two distinct â€œlinkâ€ behaviors exist threshold: Deterministic link: ui=g(𝐱⊤βu)u_i = g(\boldsymbol{x}_i^\top \beta_u) Stochastic link via link_dist = list(dist="lognormal", ...): ui∼LogNormal(μi,σu)u_i \sim \mathrm{LogNormal}(\mu_i,\sigma_u) μi=𝐱⊤βu\mu_i=\boldsymbol{x}_i^\top\beta_u mirrors common nonstationary threshold modeling ideas extremes (davison1990?; chavezdemoulin2005?), placed inside full Bayesian mixture framework (Behrens et al. 2004; donascimento2012?).

what: Backend note: SB vs CRP with covariates
title: Conditional DP Mixture Models
text: DPmixGPD supports two backends: backend = "sb": truncated stick-breaking mixture (default covariate-dependent parameters) backend = "crp": finite CRP representation conditional models, SB backend recommended. Internally, CRP backend avoids default link-mode bulk parameters deterministic, cluster-indexed covariate nodes can interfere NIMBLEâ€™s CRP samplers. want covariate links bulk parameters, generally use backend = "sb".

what: Worked example: conditional normal kernel with a GPD tail
title: Conditional DP Mixture Models
text: simulate data conditional mean depends XX, fit conditional model, generate conditional predictions. quick summary fitted object:
code: # Simulate a simple signal + heavy-tailed noise set.seed(2) beta <- c(-0.3, 0.8, -0.6)   # coefficients for (Intercept, x1, x2) mu <- drop(X %*% beta) y <- mu + rt(N, df = 3)     # heavy-tailed noise  mcmc <- list(niter = 400, nburnin = 100, thin = 2, nchains = 1, seed = 1)  bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = mcmc )  fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle, show_progress = FALSE, quiet = TRUE) ## Defining model ##   [Note] Registering 'dNormGpd' as a distribution based on its use in BUGS code. title: Conditional DP Mixture Models
text: conditional models, predict() uses x prediction design matrix XpredX_{\text{pred}}. type = "density" "survival", must also provide y grid outcome values function evaluated. returned objects contain point estimates uncertainty summaries. example: can also plot prediction objects directly:   (Plots enabled vignette build; faster local iteration can temporarily set chunk eval=FALSE.)
code: # Prediction points: two covariate profiles newdat <- data.frame(x1 = c(-1, 1), x2 = c(0, 1)) Xpred <- model.matrix(~ x1 + x2, newdat) Xpred[, "x1"] <- scale(Xpred[, "x1"])  # apply same scaling convention  ygrid <- seq(quantile(y, 0.01), quantile(y, 0.99), length.out = 120)  dens_pred <- predict(fit, x = Xpred, y = ygrid, type = "density", cred.level = 0.95) q_pred <- predict(fit, x = Xpred, type = "quantile", index = c(0.5, 0.9, 0.95), cred.level = 0.95) # Quantile predictions for each covariate profile q_pred$fit ##   estimate index id  lower  upper ## 1 -0.67096  0.50  1 -0.956 -0.403 ## 2  0.00531  0.50  2 -0.299  0.315 ## 3  0.97010  0.90  1  0.507  1.598 ## 4  2.00828  0.90  2  1.315  2.720 ## 5  1.64707  0.95  1  0.899  2.620 ## 6  2.85975  0.95  2  1.995  4.021 plot(dens_pred) plot(q_pred)

title: DPmixGPD: Unconditional bulkâ€“tail density estimation
text: covariates, bulk model ordinary Dirichlet process mixture: fDPM(y)=∑j=1∞wjk(y∣Θj), f_{\mathrm{DPM}}(y) \;=\; \sum_{j=1}^{\infty} w_j \, k(y\mid \Theta_j),  DP stick-breaking weights wjw_j(Sethuraman 1994) blocked Gibbs truncation J=𝚌𝚘𝚖𝚙𝚘𝚗𝚎𝚗𝚝𝚜J=\texttt{components}(Ishwaran James 2001). practice, DPmixGPD uses finite representation SB CRP backends, controlled single argument components. GPD=TRUE, DPmixGPD fits (upper) tail using generalized Pareto exceedance model couples bulk via proper bulkâ€“tail splice (see â€œModel specificationâ€¦â€ vignette full density/CDF). joint construction spirit Bayesian semiparametric bulkâ€“tail models avoid fixing hard threshold instead estimate bulk tail together (Frigessi et al. 2002; Nascimento et al. 2012; Behrens et al. 2004).

title: DPmixGPD: Unconditional bulkâ€“tail density estimation
text: good kernel choice driven support first shape second: outcome supported ℝ\mathbb{R}: Normal / Laplace / Cauchy kernels natural. outcome supported ℝ+\mathbb{R}_+: Gamma / Lognormal / Inverse-Gaussian / Amoroso kernels typically appropriate. data heavy-tailed already bulk, flexible positive-support kernel (e.g., Lognormal, Amoroso) may absorb structure lighter kernel push tail, can affect tail stability. DPmixGPD ships registry describing kernels support features.

title: DPmixGPD: Unconditional bulkâ€“tail density estimation
text: DPmixGPD supports backend="sb" (stick-breaking) backend="crp" (Chinese restaurant process). correspond DP prior expose different computational representations (Neal 2000). Practical guidance unconditional case: Start backend="sb" want stable finite representation explicit truncation level JJ. Use backend="crp" prefer partition-driven representation (often convenient conceptually clustering), noting finite NIMBLE model still uses components maximum representable number clusters.

title: DPmixGPD: Unconditional bulkâ€“tail density estimation
text: decision GPD=TRUE/FALSE cosmetic; changes likelihood posterior targets. GPD=FALSE fits pure DPM density full support. appropriate mainly want flexible density estimation without explicit extreme extrapolation. GPD=TRUE fits joint bulkâ€“tail model exceedances beyond threshold modeled GPD total density remains proper splicing. appropriate want stable extreme quantiles tail probabilities beyond observed bulk. Even unconditional case, tail inference coupled bulk via survival mass threshold SDPM(u)=1−FDPM(u)S_{\mathrm{DPM}}(u)=1-F_{\mathrm{DPM}}(u). exactly model tends stable â€œfit bulk, fit tailâ€ pipelines exceedance sample size small.

what: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
title: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
text: family splices generalized Pareto distribution (GPD) threshold threshold onto Amoroso mixture bulk. Let \(F_{mix}\) denote Amoroso mixture CDF. spliced CDF \(F(x)=F_{mix}(x)\) \(x<threshold\) \(F(x)=F_{mix}(threshold) + \left\{1-F_{mix}(threshold)\right\}G(x)\) \(x\ge threshold\), \(G\) GPD CDF exceedances threshold.

NULL, uses training design (stored). type Prediction type: "density": Posterior predictive density f(y | x, data) "survival": Posterior predictive survival S(y | x, data) = 1 - F(y | x, data) "quantile": Posterior predictive quantiles Q(p | x, data) "sample": Posterior predictive samples Y^rep ~ f(y | x, data) "mean": Posterior predictive mean E(Y | x, data) (averaged posterior parameter uncertainty) "rmean": Posterior predictive restricted mean \(E[\min(Y, cutoff) \mid x, data]\) "median": Posterior predictive median (quantile p=0.5) "location": Alias "mean" "fit": Per-observation posterior predictive draws Note: type="mean" returns posterior predictive mean, integrates parameter uncertainty. differs mean single model distribution. p Numeric vector probabilities quantiles (required type="quantile"). index Alias p; numeric vector quantile levels. nsim Number posterior predictive samples (type="sample"). cred.level Credible level credible intervals (default 0.95 95 percent intervals). interval Character NULL; type credible interval: NULL interval, "credible" equal-tailed quantile intervals (default), "hpd" highest posterior density intervals. probs Quantiles credible interval bands. store_draws Logical; whether store posterior draws (type="sample"). nsim_mean Number posterior predictive samples use posterior mean estimation (type="mean"). cutoff Finite numeric cutoff type="rmean" (restricted mean). ncores Number CPU cores use parallel prediction (supported). ... Unused.

title: Predict from a MixGPD fit — predict.mixgpd_fit
text: list elements: fit: data frame estimate/lower/upper columns (posterior means draws) plus index columns (e.g. id, y, index). lower, upper: reserved backward compatibility (typically NULL). type, grid: metadata.

title: Predict from a MixGPD fit — predict.mixgpd_fit
text: object stores dedicated predictive machinery, can keep signature swap internals without breaking user code. type="mean" option computes posterior predictive mean : posterior draw s, computing E(Y | x, theta_s) via Monte Carlo simulation Averaging conditional means posterior: E(Y | x, data) = mean_s(E(Y | x, theta_s)) accounts parameter uncertainty Bayesian predictive mean. tail shape parameter xi >= 1 (heavy tail), mean undefined function returns Inf warning suggesting alternatives like median restricted mean.

title: Predict from a MixGPD fit — predict.mixgpd_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ y <- abs(stats::rnorm(50)) + 0.1 bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(y = y, backend = "sb", kernel = "normal",                              GPD = TRUE, components = 6,                              mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1)) fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle) pr <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9)) pr_surv <- predict(fit, y = sort(y), type = "survival") pr_cdf <- list(fit = 1 - pr_surv$fit) # HPD intervals pr_hpd <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9), interval = "hpd") # No intervals pr_none <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9), interval = NULL) # Restricted mean (finite under heavy tails) pr_rmean <- predict(fit, type = "rmean", cutoff = 10, interval = "credible") } # }

"bayes_mean": Bayesian PIT using draw-wise CDFs averaged draws. "bayes_draw": Bayesian PIT using single draw-wise CDF per observation. Bayesian PIT modes drop invalid posterior draws using validation rules prediction attach diagnostics via attr(res, "pit_diagnostics"). pit_seed Optional integer seed reproducible bayes_draw sampling. ... code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ y <- abs(stats::rnorm(50)) + 0.1 X <- data.frame(x1 = stats::rnorm(50), x2 = stats::runif(50)) bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(y = y, X = X, backend = "sb", kernel = "lognormal",                              GPD = FALSE, components = 4,                              mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1)) fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle) pit_plugin <- residuals(fit, type = "pit", pit = "plugin") pit_bayes_mean <- residuals(fit, type = "pit", pit = "bayes_mean", pit_seed = 1L) pit_bayes_draw <- residuals(fit, type = "pit", pit = "bayes_draw", pit_seed = 1L) attr(pit_bayes_draw, "pit_diagnostics") } # }
